title: Typhoon jets based at RAF Coningsby playing key role as Iran conflict escalates

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Sir Keir Starmer said RAF Typhoon jets had been patrolling the skies around Qatar as part of a defensive operation after the conflict with Iran escalated. The Ministry of Defence later said an RAF Typhoon operating from Qatar with the joint UK-Qatar Typhoon Squadron shot down an Iranian drone heading towards Qatari territory on Sunday 1 March.

sections.heading: Interception details
sections.content: The MoD said the aircraft was on a defensive air patrol and used an air-to-air missile to destroy the drone. It described this as the joint squadron's first operational interception in defence of Qatar. Armed Forces Minister Al Carns publicly praised the RAF after the interception.

sections.heading: Coningsby role
sections.content: RAF Coningsby is one of the RAF's two main Quick Reaction Alert Typhoon bases, alongside RAF Lossiemouth in Moray, and it is also the Typhoon training centre. No. 12 Squadron, the joint UK-Qatar unit based at Coningsby, was reactivated in July 2018 and integrates Qatar Emiri Air Force air and ground crews as part of Qatar's purchase of 24 Typhoons from the UK.
